meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI. Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to deliver high-end, professional designs as a solo freelancer.
content.answer_block: Eliminate design bottlenecks by leveraging AI-driven UI generation. By integrating Antigravity for rapid component scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can translate raw business requirements into polished, production-ready interfaces without manual styling. This workflow turns your Git repository into a self-evolving design engine, saving you hours of development.
content.intro:
The client meeting went perfectly, but now the screen is blank. As a freelancer, your code is clean, but your UI is a graveyard of misaligned divs and questionable color palettes. You are a developer, not a designer, and every hour spent nudging pixels is an hour stolen from your bottom line.

There is a better way to ship. By integrating Git-based AI workflows, you can treat design as a programmable asset rather than a creative burden. This guide transforms your development environment into a high-velocity design studio, allowing you to iterate faster than any agency and deliver pixel-perfect results that keep clients coming back.

steps.title: Establish the Design System Foundation
steps.description: Initialize a Tailwind-based design system so the AI understands your constraints and spacing logic before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a global Tailwind CSS configuration file that enforces a professional, modern SaaS aesthetic, including a primary color palette, consistent border-radius scaling, and type hierarchy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create tailwind.config.js with a primary brand color of #6366f1 and a professional typography scale'

steps.title: Scaffold Components via AI
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build complex, responsive components that adhere to your established design tokens, ensuring visual consistency across your repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive 'FeatureSection' component using React and Tailwind. Use a 3-column grid layout, add subtle hover effects on cards, and include Lucide icons for each feature item.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a FeatureSection component inside src/components/ that uses the design tokens defined in the config'

steps.title: Iterative Design Refinement
steps.description: Pipe user feedback or design tweaks back into Claude Code to perform surgical updates to your UI without breaking existing functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current UI. Increase white space, improve the contrast ratio for accessibility, and add a subtle drop shadow to all interactive elements to improve visual depth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update all card components to use shadow-lg and increase padding-y to 8 to improve visual breathing room'

steps.title: Sync Design States with Git
steps.description: Commit your AI-generated design iterations with semantic messages to track the evolution of your UI, ensuring you can roll back if a design experiment fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a semantic commit message that summarizes the recent UI overhaul, specifically highlighting the accessibility and spacing improvements made.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat(ui): implement refined spacing and accessibility updates via AI'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Deploy an AI-check script to verify that your auto-generated UI matches your design system requirements before merging to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that scans the codebase for hardcoded hex colors and suggests migrating them to the Tailwind config file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scan for hardcoded colors and refactor them to use the defined theme variables'

pro_tips: Always define a 'design token' file first; AI works 10x better when it has a central source of truth for styles.
Use Claude Code's '--verbose' flag when prototyping to debug why the AI chose specific layout structures.
Create a 'style-guide.md' in your root directory; point the AI to this file in every prompt to maintain consistency.
Leverage Git branches for 'Design Experiments'—never push AI-generated UI changes directly to main without a visual review.
Ask the AI to explain its design choices; this helps you learn UI principles while you ship.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, start building a library of reusable AI-generated components. Store these in a private Git repository to create your own 'freelancer component store' that you can pull into every future client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on design and start delivering high-end products that command higher rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
